label: Australian tax parameters for revenue estimation

income_tax.label: Individual income tax brackets (residents, 2025-26)
income_tax.note: Stage 3 tax cuts effective 1 July 2024. Medicare levy of 2% applies on top. Low Income Tax Offset (LITO) not modelled.

superannuation.label: Superannuation guarantee
superannuation.note: 12% from 1 July 2025. Employer contribution on top of salary.

gst.label: Goods and Services Tax
gst.note: 10% flat rate on most goods and services. Fresh food, health, education exempt.

payroll_tax.label: Payroll tax by state/territory
payroll_tax.note: Payroll tax is a state tax with different rates and thresholds. Rates and thresholds for 2025-26.

VIC.note: 4.85% above AUD 900k. Mental health levy adds 0.5% above AUD 10m.

QLD.note: 4.75% above AUD 1.3m. Higher rate of 4.95% above AUD 6.5m.

TAS.note: 4% above AUD 1.25m. Higher rate 6.1% above AUD 2m.

ACT.note: 4.85% above AUD 2m. Higher rate 6.85% above AUD 5m.

company_tax.label: Corporate tax rate
company_tax.note: 25% for base rate entities (aggregated turnover < AUD 50m). 30% for all others.

effective_tax_wedge.label: Simplified effective tax rate for IO revenue estimation
effective_tax_wedge.description: Approximate combined effective tax rate on additional employment income, for quick revenue estimation from IO impact results.

low_income.note: Income tax ~14% + Medicare 2% + Super 12% (employer cost, not employee tax, but represents fiscal flow)

median_income.note: Income tax ~19% + Medicare 2% + employer super cost

effective_tax_wedge.note: These are rough estimates for IO revenue impact reporting. They include income tax and Medicare levy only (not super, which is an employer cost not a tax receipt in the traditional sense). For precise estimates, model each bracket.

source.publication: Australian Taxation Office, State Revenue Offices
